ps-1.value: Musicians need a fast, cloud-synced tablature editor that works across guitar, bass, and drums without desktop software.

ps-2.value: Hobbyist guitarists, band members, and music teachers who write and share tabs

ps-4.value: Guitar Pro ($70 desktop app), Ultimate Guitar's clunky editor, or plain text tabs in Notepad

vp-1.value: A free, browser-based tab editor with cloud sync, public sharing, and multi-instrument support.

vp-2.value: Not a DAW, not a sheet music notation tool, not a tab archive/library like Ultimate Guitar

vp-3.value: The go-to free online tab editor with a growing community sharing original tabs and arrangements

cl-1.value: Guitar Pro (powerful but $70, desktop only), Songsterr (playback-focused, no real editor), Ultimate Guitar (tab archive, weak editor), Flat.io (notation-focused, not tab-native)

cl-2.value: Free and browser-based, multi-instrument (guitar/bass/drums in one project), instant public sharing via slug URLs

cl-3.value: Guitar Pro is expensive and desktop-only, UG's editor is clunky and ad-heavy, Songsterr is read-only for most users

vr-1.value: Personal experience writing tabs for band rehearsals, Reddit threads in r/guitar and r/Bass requesting free browser-based tab editors

vr-2.value: No good free browser-based editor, no cloud sync in existing tools, sharing tabs requires file exports

vr-3.value: If users create and share tabs without prompting, the core loop works

sd-1.value: Tab editor (guitar/bass/drums), cloud sync, sharing, playback, click track, PDF export, practice mode, power chords, palm mute annotations, library management, offline support, theming

sd-3.value: Tab editor with cell-based input, cloud sync via Supabase, public sharing via slug URLs, playback with click track, project library

sd-4.value: Collaboration/real-time editing, tab marketplace, audio import/transcription, MIDI export

rc-2.value: Free: 3 projects, no sharing. Paid ($29 lifetime): unlimited projects, cloud sync, sharing, PDF export

rc-4.value: Supabase Pro + custom domain: $35/mo, Vercel: free tier, SendGrid: free tier. Total burn: ~$35/mo

lc-1.value: Privacy policy created at /privacy route — covers data collection, third-party services, user rights, cookies

lc-3.value: No conflicts found — 'Heavy Tabs' is unique in the music software space

lc-4.value: App collects email via auth, stores user data in Supabase (US region). No EU-specific data processing. Basic GDPR compliance via Supabase's DPA.

us-1.value: 123 user stories documented in TESTING.md covering editor, library, sharing, playback, auth, offline, practice mode, PDF export, and palm mute annotations

us-2.value: Each story in TESTING.md has specific acceptance criteria with expected behavior

ux-1.value: Main editor (/), project library (drawer), public viewer (/tab/:slug), style guide (/styleguide), auth callback, landing page

ux-2.value: Land on editor with empty tab, create content, prompted to sign in when saving/sharing

ux-3.value: Open project from library, edit tab cells, save to cloud, share via slug URL

ux-4.value: Offline fallback to localStorage, migration dialog for local-to-cloud, empty states, auth session persistence

vd-1.value: Custom component system (UiButton, UiInput, UiSelect, UiCheckbox) with SCSS tokens and mixins

vd-2.value: Dark-first with light/dark theme toggle, SCSS design tokens

vd-3.value: BarGrid, Part, TabEditorNew, Library, ShareModal, AuthModal, PracticeMode, PageFooter with legend

si-1.value: React 18, TypeScript, Vite, React Router, Sass (SCSS modules), Lucide React icons

si-3.value: Vercel (auto-deploys from GitHub main branch), custom domain heavytabs.app

si-5.value: Vite for fast dev, React for interactive editor UI, Supabase for auth + real-time sync + RLS

da-1.value: profiles (auto-created on signup), projects (tab data as JSONB), shared_links (8-char slugs with view counts)

da-2.value: Supabase Pro (Postgres + Auth + RLS), project ref mffldntdtsqckmhuwylm, custom domain api.heavytabs.app

da-3.value: Direct Supabase client calls (no API routes), RLS policies for row-level access control

da-4.value: SQL migrations in supabase/migrations/, localStorage-to-cloud migration dialog for existing users

aa-2.value: Google OAuth + magic link (passwordless) via Supabase Auth, SendGrid for emails from noreply@heavytabs.app

aa-3.value: Single role: authenticated user owns their projects, RLS enforces access

t-6.value: 596KB bundle, live on Vercel with Analytics. Performance acceptable for production use.

cs-3.value: Auth errors, sync failures, and offline fallback messages handled

cs-5.value: SEO planned for 'free tab editor', 'online tab maker'

plc-5.value: Vercel built-in rollback — revert to any previous deployment via dashboard

sm-4.value: App is live in production at heavytabs.app with paying users. Initial monitoring period complete.
